data.executiveSummary: AI fitness coaching enters an oversaturated market dominated by well-funded players like Future ($75M raised), Freeletics ($70M), and Fitbod. While the market is large, CAC is prohibitively expensive ($150-300) and retention is poor (40-60% annual churn). Verdict: No-go unless targeting an extremely specific underserved niche.
pivotSuggestions: AI-powered physical therapy and injury prevention for amateur athletes
Corporate wellness platform with team-based AI coaching
Specialized fitness AI for pregnant/postpartum women
opportunities: Integration with Strava API for runners/cyclists could provide organic distribution
Workplace wellness programs offer B2B2C distribution channel
Partner with boutique gym chains lacking digital offerings
Target specific conditions (e.g., lower back pain) for medical referrals
Build community features around specific sports/activities
risks: Extremely high CAC ($150+ per user) in fitness app marketplace
Low retention - most fitness apps see 80% drop-off within 2 months
Major players (Peloton, Apple Fitness+) have massive content budgets
AI coaching still can't match human trainers for form correction
App Store policies limit subscription pricing power

regulatoryAndAiRisk.complianceBurden: Medium - Liability concerns for exercise guidance
regulatoryAndAiRisk.aiTakeoverRisk: High - Apple/Google likely to add AI coaching natively
regulatoryAndAiRisk.details: Major platforms could easily implement AI coaching features, while liability for AI-generated exercise advice remains murky.

competitors.name: Future
competitors.keyWeakness: High price point ($150/mo) limits mass market adoption

competitors.name: Apple Fitness+
competitors.keyWeakness: Requires Apple Watch and ecosystem lock-in

dimensionExplanations.marketDemand: Global fitness app market is $14.7B (2023) but extremely fragmented. User acquisition costs have risen 300% since 2019 due to iOS privacy changes and increased competition.
dimensionExplanations.competitiveIntensity: Oversaturated market with 50+ well-funded players and platform risks from Apple/Google. No clear moat potential beyond brand/community.
dimensionExplanations.technicalFeasibility: Core MVP is achievable in 3-4 months, but effective AI form correction requires significant R&D and data collection.
dimensionExplanations.monetizationClarity: Standard subscription model but high churn (60%+ annually) requires aggressive pricing to achieve positive LTV/CAC.
dimensionExplanations.founderFit: Requires deep expertise in fitness programming, ML deployment, and content production - challenging for solo founder.
dimensionExplanations.timing: Market maturity and iOS privacy changes make this a particularly challenging time for fitness app growth.

data.opportunityGapSummary: Success requires targeting underserved niches with specific pain points (e.g., postpartum, injury recovery) where AI can provide unique value and word-of-mouth distribution. Mass market approach is suicide given CAC dynamics.

marketSizing.sourceLogic: TAM based on global fitness app market, SAM focuses on premium segment, SOM assumes 1% capture of premium market given competition.

marketDemand: Global fitness app downloads grew 46% in 2020 but plateaued since
87% of users prefer hybrid (digital + in-person) fitness solutions
Average fitness app user retention is 29% after 90 days
competitiveIntensity: Top 10 fitness apps capture 87% of revenue
iOS privacy changes increased CAC by 200-300%
Major platforms investing heavily in fitness features
technicalFeasibility: Form correction requires sophisticated computer vision
Existing pose estimation APIs have 15-20% error rate
Content creation needs significant ongoing investment
monetizationClarity: CAC ranges from $150-300 per paid user
founderFit: Requires ML expertise for pose estimation
timing: Rising CAC due to privacy changes
